name: Funnel Hub
lengthClass: long-form (multi-page website, not a linear funnel)

goals: Give a personal brand or company a single authoritative home that looks like a real website to press/PR/partners while every internal link still routes into a front-end funnel.
Capture 'shadow funnel' traffic — the organic research journey people take after seeing an ad or hearing the name.
Organize all of a marketer's funnels, offers, blog posts and social content in one navigable place (a Value Ladder map).
keywords: blog + social + funnels in one place
signals: Client already has multiple funnels/offers and needs a top-level brand home that ties them together.
Client is running paid ads and wants to capture people who Google their name/brand afterward.
Client needs a credible 'website' for media kits, partnerships, or guest appearances but does not want a dead-end brochure site.
antiSignals: Single-offer business with no other funnels yet — start with a squeeze/lead or front-end funnel first.
Goal is a direct-response sale right now — use a Sales Letter / VSL / Webinar funnel, not the Hub.

pageStructure.purpose: Pass the 5-second 'grunt test': instantly communicate who this is for, what they get, and the one primary next step. Routes the majority of traffic into the #1 front-end funnel.
elements: Brand logo + clean top navigation (Start Here, About, Content/Blog, Products, Contact)
Hero: StoryBrand one-liner headline + subhead (problem -> plan -> success)
Primary CTA button into the #1 front-end funnel (e.g. lead magnet / book / webinar)
Trust bar: 'as seen in' / press logos / follower counts
Value-ladder section: cards linking to each front-end funnel (not a generic shop)
Featured content / recent blog teasers
Email-capture band (secondary opt-in)

pageStructure.purpose: Build the relationship and authority through origin story; convert curiosity into a follow + opt-in.
elements: Founder photo + Epiphany Bridge origin story
Manifesto / mission statement (cause people can identify with)
Attractive Character backstory, character flaws, and polarity
Social-follow buttons (the shadow-funnel anchors)
Inline opt-in to the signature lead magnet

pageStructure.purpose: Orient a first-time visitor and route them to the correct front-end funnel for their stage.
elements: Short framing video or copy block (Hook-Story-Offer)
Tiered cards: free lead magnet -> low-ticket -> core offer -> high-ticket
Each card CTA links OUT to its own dedicated front-end funnel (never a shared cart)

pageStructure.purpose: Feed organic + shadow-funnel search traffic, demonstrate expertise, and recycle every post into an opt-in.
elements: Blog RSS / post grid integration
In-article + end-of-article CTAs into the relevant lead funnel
Category navigation aligned to the Dream 100 topics
Newsletter opt-in sidebar/footer

pageStructure.purpose: Reinforce credibility for the shadow funnel — when someone researches the brand, owned properties dominate the result.
elements: Embedded social streams (Instagram/YouTube/podcast)
Press mentions + media kit download (gated opt-in optional)
Speaker / collaboration / podcast-guest CTA

pageStructure.purpose: Window-shopping index where every product links into its own funnel rather than a generic product page.
elements: Product cards with image + one-line promise
Each card routes to its dedicated VSL / sales-letter / order funnel
No dead ends — every path ends in an opt-in or order step

copyFramework.primaryPersona: Donald Miller — StoryBrand persona (The Brand Clarifier): the SB7 framework — (1) a Character who (2) has a Problem, (3) meets a Guide, (4) who gives them a Plan, (5) and calls them to Action, (6) that ends in Success and (7) helps them avoid Failure — plus the one-liner and the grunt test.
supportingPersonas: Russell Brunson — Traffic Secrets persona (The Traffic Strategist): Dream 100, Hook-Story-Offer for native content, the Funnel Hub + Shadow Funnel capture doctrine.

Russell Brunson — Expert Secrets persona (The Attractive Character / Expert): Epiphany Bridge story script, the Big Domino, false-belief patterns (vehicle/internal/external), Perfect Webinar, The Stack and Close, Hook-Story-Offer.
scripts: StoryBrand SB7 BrandScript + one-liner for the Hub Home hero (clarity > cleverness; pass the grunt test).
Epiphany Bridge (Expert Secrets) for the About/Manifesto origin story.
Hook-Story-Offer (Traffic Secrets) on every value-ladder card and content CTA.
copyFramework.notes: The Hub itself does not 'sell' — it clarifies and routes. Selling copy lives inside the front-end funnels each link points to. Keep one obvious primary CTA per page so the grunt test never fails.

pages: Build as a GHL WEBSITE (multi-page with navigation/menu), NOT a linear GHL Funnel — Hubs need cross-page nav.
Pages: Home, About/Manifesto, Start Here, Blog (GHL Blog module), Press/Social, Products index.
Wire the top nav menu so every internal link target is a Funnel step, not another static page where possible.
skill44Widgets: Skill 44 Form widget: hero/footer email opt-in (maps to a 'Hub Subscribers' tag).
Skill 44 Form widget: media-kit / newsletter gated download on Press page.
Optional Skill 44 Calendar widget on Contact for partnership/podcast bookings.
automations: Workflow: new Hub subscriber -> Soap Opera Sequence (5 emails) -> Daily Seinfeld broadcast list.
Retargeting audience pixel on all Hub pages to feed shadow-funnel recapture ads.
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 provisions the GHL Website + blog; Skill 44 instantiates the opt-in/contact widgets. Set the GHL Website domain to the root brand domain so branded searches land here.
